text: Roe v. Wade ruling extends bodily autonomy precedent later cited in LGBTQ+ rights cases
San Francisco begins issuing same-sex marriage licenses under Mayor Gavin Newsom
Arizona governor vetoes anti-LGBTQ SB 1062 after national pushback
Civil rights organizer Bayard Rustin is born in West Chester, Pennsylvania
International Transgender Day of Visibility is first observed
Iowa Supreme Court unanimously legalizes same-sex marriage in Varnum v. Brien
Vermont becomes the first US state to legally recognize civil unions
WHO removes homosexuality from the ICD; later observed as IDAHOBIT
Harvey Milk, pioneering openly gay elected official, is born in Woodmere, New York
Pulse nightclub shooting in Orlando claims 49 lives at a Latin night gathering
New York legalizes same-sex marriage with the Marriage Equality Act
Lawrence v. Texas strikes down US sodomy laws, decriminalizing same-sex intimacy
Obergefell v. Hodges nationalizes marriage equality across the United States
Stonewall Uprising begins in NYC, catalyzing the modern LGBTQ+ movement
First Pride marches held in NYC, LA, and Chicago, marking one year since Stonewall
Gilbert Baker's rainbow flag debuts at San Francisco's Gay Freedom Day parade
The New York Times publishes the first major report on a 'rare cancer' later identified as AIDS
Truman signs Executive Order 9981 desegregating the military, a foundation for later DADT repeal
President Clinton signs executive order banning anti-gay discrimination in security clearances
Activist and Stonewall figure Marsha P. Johnson is born in Elizabeth, New Jersey
'Don't Ask, Don't Tell' is officially repealed, allowing open service in the US military
Defense of Marriage Act (DOMA) is signed into law, later struck down in 2013
First International Bisexual Visibility Day is observed
Matthew Shepard is fatally attacked in Wyoming, spurring federal hate crimes legislation
Second National March on Washington for Lesbian and Gay Rights draws hundreds of thousands
First National March on Washington for Lesbian and Gay Rights
Audre Lorde delivers her landmark 'I Am Your Sister' address
Harvey Milk is elected to the San Francisco Board of Supervisors
First Transgender Day of Remembrance honors victims of anti-trans violence
Harvey Milk and Mayor George Moscone are assassinated at San Francisco City Hall
First World AIDS Day observed by the WHO to honor lives lost to the pandemic
American Psychiatric Association removes homosexuality from the DSM
US Senate votes to repeal 'Don't Ask, Don't Tell', ending the policy
President Obama signs the 'Don't Ask, Don't Tell' Repeal Act into law
Queer Nation is founded in New York, reclaiming 'queer' as a political identity
AIDS activist group ACT UP is founded in New York City
Ellen DeGeneres comes out on the cover of Time and on her sitcom
California Supreme Court rules same-sex couples have the right to marry
First lesbian Pride march held in Washington, D.C.
Author and civil rights icon James Baldwin is born in Harlem, New York
First openly gay person, Robert Eichberg, addresses the National Gay Rights March
Matthew Shepard dies from his injuries; vigils sweep the nation
California voters narrowly pass Prop 8, banning same-sex marriage; sparks national activism
Australia legalizes same-sex marriage after a national postal survey
Activist and Pose star Marsha P. Johnson cofounds STAR with Sylvia Rivera (movement era)
Mary Shelley is born; later scholarship recognizes queer themes throughout her work
CDC reports first cases of what would become known as AIDS
California becomes first state to require LGBTQ history in public school curriculum
Taiwan's first same-sex couples marry under new law, an Asian first
Canada's Ontario court rules existing marriage definition unconstitutional, paving the way for nationwide equality
